branch_suggestions.reason: Top alignment with your interest in technology, coding, and software development.

limitations: Your rank (60,000) is above recent cutoff ranges for CSE branches in top-tier Government Colleges.
High competition for Computer Science leaves very limited seat availability for ranks above 15,000.
At a rank of 60,000, securing seats in top-20 colleges for core branches via Convenor Quota is highly competitive.
suggestions: Keep CSE as a target, but prioritize adding Private Autonomous colleges on your web options.
Explore related specializations like AI & ML, Data Science, or Information Technology (IT) which typically have slightly higher rank cutoffs.
Consider newer branches or local region colleges where cutoff ranks are more relaxed.
We recommend listing 25+ colleges in your web options list during counseling to avoid missing out on seat allocation.
Include colleges in tier-3 cities or newer colleges in your options list (e.g. Santhiram, Anantha Lakshmi).
Look into branches like ECE, EEE, or Mech which have cutoffs extending beyond 40,000-60,000.
Explore management quota (B-Category seats) options if your budget allows (typically ₹1.5L to ₹2.5L per year).

dream.name: Andhra University College of Engineering (Autonomous), Visakhapatnam
dream.scholarships: Merit-based scholarships, full Jagananna Vidya Deevena fee reimbursement eligible, sports quota waiver
dream.explain_why: Aspirational college. Cutoff rank (850) is highly competitive relative to your rank (60000). However, it offers stellar placements (Highest: ₹32.0 LPA, Avg: ₹7.5 LPA) and top-tier infrastructure. Recommend putting this high on your web options list in case of second-round seat availability.

However, it offers stellar placements (Highest: ₹28.0 LPA, Avg: ₹6.8 LPA) and top-tier infrastructure. Recommend putting this high on your web options list in case of second-round seat availability.

dream.explain_why: Aspirational college. Cutoff rank (2000) is highly competitive relative to your rank (60000). next_steps.title: Counseling Registration
next_steps.description: Pay processing fee online on the APSCHE EAPCET website once the official schedule is released.

next_steps.title: Certificate Verification
next_steps.description: Verification of uploaded documents online. Visit helpline centers only if discrepancies are flagged.

next_steps.title: Web Option Entry
next_steps.description: Log in to the counseling portal and enter college-branch codes. Put all 'Dream' options first, followed by 'Target', and then 'Safe' backups.

next_steps.title: Seat Allotment
next_steps.description: Review the allotted college based on rank and reservation rules. You can opt for 'Self-Reporting' while participating in Phase 2.

next_steps.title: Physical Reporting
next_steps.description: Visit the allotted college with original certificates, allotment order, and join within the specified timeline.
